title: Arum neglectum
taxonomy: Arum neglectum is classified in the kingdom Plantae and the family Araceae. It belongs to the genus Arum, which includes several species of flowering plants. The species Arum neglectum is a member of this genus and is native to certain regions. Arum neglectum is a type of perennial plant, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and growth habits. Further classification of Arum neglectum may be refined based on ongoing botanical research and classification updates.

intro: Arum neglectum, also known as the "Wild Calla", is a perennial plant species belonging to the Arum genus, characterized by its distinctive, hood-like spathe and white or pale yellow spadix. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Arum neglectum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Arum neglectum is traditionally prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used in some herbal remedies in the form of a syrup or decoction.
